Reason_for_Visit.summary: The patient is a 63-year-old female with invasive carcinoma with ductal and lobular features, and high-grade DCIS (ER negative/weakly PR positive), for a discussion of treatment options for recently diagnosed breast cancer.

Cancer_Diagnosis.Type_of_Cancer: ER+ (>95%)/PR (60%)/HER2 2+ by IHC and negative by FISH 5-10% invasive carcinoma with ductal and lobular features, ER negative/weakly PR + (1%) high grade DCIS

Lab_Results.lab_summary: Glucose, meter download: 105 mg/dL, Glucose, meter download: 185 mg/dL, Glucose, meter download: 236 mg/dL, Glucose, meter download: 116 mg/dL

Clinical_Findings.findings: Pathology results: 1 cm grade 2 invasive lobular carcinoma, 0.7 cm invasive carcinoma with ductal and lobular features, intermediate and high grade DCIS, negative margins. Physical exam: left breast mastectomy scar healing well with tissue expander in place, no dominant masses in right breast, no suspicious skin changes overlying expander. No palpable cervical or supraclavicular adenopathy.

Current_Medications.current_meds: acetaminophen (TYLENOL), atorvastatin (LIPITOR), cholecalciferol, docusate sodium (COLACE), ibuprofen (ADVIL,MOTRIN), LORazepam (ATIVAN), MULTIVIT-MINERALS/FERROUS FUM, oxyCODONE (ROXICODONE), sulfamethoxazole-trimethoprim (BACTRIM DS,SEPTRA DS)

Treatment_Goals.response_assessment: Not yet on treatment — no response to assess.
Treatment_Goals.current_meds: acetaminophen, atorvastatin, cholecalciferol, docusate sodium, ibuprofen, LORazepam, MULTIVIT-MINERALS/FERROUS FUM, oxyCODONE, sulfamethoxazole-trimethoprim

Response_Assessment.response_assessment: The patient's cancer is currently being assessed as low risk, with no evidence of tumor in the sentinel lymph node (0/1) and negative margins.

hormonal therapy.summary: will receive at least 5 years of adjuvant hormonal therapy
hormonal therapy.Long term side_effects_discussed: possible acceleration of bone density loss

other treatment.summary: will maintain a calcium rich diet, will take in an adequate amount of Vitamin D (typically 600-800 IU per day), will engage in weight bearing exercise

Medication_Plan.medication_plan: will receive letrozole, an aromatase inhibitor, for at least 5 years
Medication_Plan.current_meds: Not yet on treatment — no response to assess.
Medication_Plan.response_assessment: Not yet on treatment — no response to assess.

Therapy_plan.therapy_plan: an endocrine based therapy with letrozole, specifically an aromatase inhibitor
Therapy_plan.exercise: weight bearing exercise for 150 minutes of moderate intensity per week
